pi_items.source: Task specification in task.json and README.md; verified against electrical engineering calculations
Task specification in task.json and README.md; verified against electrical engineering calculations
Task specification in task.json and README.md; verified by calculating P/S = 4186/6440 = 0.65
Calculated using S = V × I = 230 × 28 = 6,440 VA; standard single-phase power formula
Calculated using P = V × I × PF = 230 × 28 × 0.65 = 4,186 W; standard real power formula
Calculated using Q = S × sin(arccos(PF)) = 6440 × sin(49.46°) = 6440 × 0.7599 ≈ 4,894 VAR; standard reactive power formula
Verifier uses this tolerance in _number_near() function to check if calculated values are within 3% of expected values; reasonable for electrical measurements
Verified via web search as the most common power factor threshold used by utility companies for industrial and commercial customers; confirmed in multiple sources on power factor penalties

privileged_info_summary: Single-phase power quality audit task using a 230V, 28A circuit with 0.65 power factor (lagging). All electrical calculations are verified using standard IEEE formulas: Apparent Power = 6,440 VA (V×I), Real Power = 4,186 W (V×I×PF), Reactive Power = 4,894 VAR (S×sin(arccos(PF))). The power factor of 0.65 is below the utility penalty threshold of 0.85, which is a real-world standard used by utility companies for charging penalties. The task uses the com.hsn.electricalcalculations Android app to perform the calculations. All tolerance values (3%) and test parameters are consistent with electrical engineering practices.
